title: ESM Format
description: EarthSciML Serialization Format (v0.1.0) — a language-agnostic JSON format for Earth system model components, their composition, and runtime configuration.

models.description: ODE-based model components, keyed by unique identifier.

reaction_systems.description: Reaction network components, keyed by unique identifier.

interfaces.description: Geometric connections between domains of different dimensionality.

name.description: Short identifier for the model configuration.

Reference.description: Academic citation or data source reference.

Expression.description: Mathematical expression: a number literal, a variable/parameter reference string, or an operator node.

ExpressionNode.description: An operation in the expression AST.

args.description: Operand list. For most ops these are sub-expressions. Array ops use args for the input array operands (arrayop, broadcast, index, reshape, transpose, concat). makearray has no natural args and uses an empty array.

output_idx.description: For arrayop: the result's index signature. Each entry is either a string (a symbolic index variable like "i", "j") or the integer 1 (a literal singleton dimension for reshape/broadcast). Mirrors SymbolicUtils.ArrayOp.output_idx.

expr.description: For arrayop: the scalar body evaluated at each index point. May reference index symbols declared in output_idx as well as additional (contracted) index symbols. Mirrors SymbolicUtils.ArrayOp.expr.

reduce.description: For arrayop: the reduction operator applied to any index symbol that appears in expr but not in output_idx. Default is "+".

ranges.description: For arrayop: optional map from index symbol name to the range it iterates over. Each value is either a 2-element array [start, stop] (unit step) or a 3-element array [start, step, stop]. Indices not present are inferred from the domain / operand shapes at runtime. Mirrors SymbolicUtils.ArrayOp.ranges.

regions.description: For makearray: list of sub-region boxes of the output array. Each region is an array of [start, stop] pairs, one per output dimension. The nth region is filled with the nth entry of values. Overlapping regions are permitted; later regions overwrite earlier ones. Mirrors SymbolicUtils.ArrayMaker.regions.

values.description: For makearray: list of expressions, one per entry in regions. Each value may be a scalar expression (broadcast across the region) or an array-valued expression whose shape matches the region (excluding singleton dimensions). Mirrors SymbolicUtils.ArrayMaker.values.

shape.description: For reshape: the target shape. Each entry is either an integer (a concrete length) or a string (a symbolic dimension reference).

perm.description: For transpose: optional axis permutation. A list of 0-based axis indices giving the new order. If omitted, the matrix-transpose convention is used (reverse axes).

axis.description: For concat: the 0-based axis along which to concatenate the operand arrays. All operands must have identical shape on every other axis.

fn.description: For broadcast: the name of the scalar operator to apply element-wise to the operands in args. Must be an ExpressionNode op name drawn from the scalar subset (arithmetic, elementary functions, comparisons, etc.).

Equation.description: An equation: lhs = rhs (or lhs ~ rhs in MTK notation).

AffectEquation.description: An affect equation in an event: lhs is the target variable (string), rhs is an expression.

lhs.description: Target variable name (value after the event).

rhs.description: Expression for the new value. Use Pre(var) to reference pre-event values.

ContinuousEvent.description: Fires when a condition expression crosses zero (root-finding). Maps to MTK SymbolicContinuousCallback.

conditions.description: Expressions that trigger the event when they cross zero.

affects.description: Affect equations applied on positive-going zero crossings (or both directions if affect_neg is absent). Empty array for pure detection.

affect_neg.description: Separate affects for negative-going zero crossings. If null or absent, affects is used for both directions.

reinitialize.description: Whether to reinitialize the system after the event.

DiscreteEventTrigger.description: Trigger specification for a discrete event.

oneOf.description: Fires when the boolean expression is true at the end of a timestep.

initial_offset.description: Offset from t=0 for the first firing.

times.description: Array of simulation times at which to fire.

FunctionalAffect.description: A registered functional affect handler for complex event behavior that cannot be expressed symbolically.

handler_id.description: Registered identifier for the affect implementation.

read_vars.description: State variables accessed by the handler.

modified_params.description: Parameters modified by the handler (implicitly discrete parameters).

DiscreteEvent.description: Fires when a boolean condition is true at end of a timestep, or at preset/periodic times. Maps to MTK SymbolicDiscreteCallback.

affects.description: Affect equations. Required unless functional_affect is used.

functional_affect.description: Registered functional affect handler (alternative to symbolic affects).

discrete_parameters.description: Parameters modified by this event. Required when affects modify parameters rather than state variables.

reinitialize.description: Whether to reinitialize the system after the event.

ModelVariable.description: A variable in an ODE model.

type.description: state = time-dependent unknown; parameter = externally set constant; observed = derived quantity.

Model.description: An ODE system — a fully specified set of time-dependent equations.

domain.description: Name of a domain from the domains section. Omit or set to null for 0D (non-spatial) models.

coupletype.description: Coupling type name. Informational label identifying this system's role in coupling.

subsystems.description: Named child models (subsystems), keyed by unique identifier. Enables hierarchical model composition. Variables in subsystems are referenced via dot notation: "ParentModel.ChildModel.var". Each subsystem can be defined inline or included by reference via a local file path or URL.

tolerance.description: Model-level default numerical tolerance for tests, used when a test or assertion does not provide its own.

tests.description: Inline validation tests that exercise this model in isolation. Each test specifies initial conditions, parameter overrides, a time span, and scalar assertions at specific (variable, time) points.

examples.description: Inline illustrative examples of how to run this model. Each example specifies initial state, parameters, a time span, an optional parameter sweep, and plot specifications.

SubsystemRef.description: A reference to an external ESM file containing a model or reaction system definition. The ref field can be a relative or absolute local file path, or an HTTP/HTTPS URL. Relative paths are resolved relative to the directory of the referencing file.

ref.description: Local file path or URL pointing to an ESM file. The referenced file must contain exactly one top-level model or reaction system, which is used as the subsystem definition.

Species.description: A reactive species in a reaction system.

Parameter.description: A parameter in a reaction system.

StoichiometryEntry.description: A species with its stoichiometric coefficient in a reaction.

Reaction.description: A single reaction in a reaction system.

substrates.description: Array of {species, stoichiometry} or null for source reactions (∅ → X).

products.description: Array of {species, stoichiometry} or null for sink reactions (X → ∅).

rate.description: Rate expression: a parameter reference string, number, or expression AST.

ReactionSystem.description: A reaction network — declarative representation of chemical or biological reactions.

domain.description: Name of a domain from the domains section. Omit or set to null for 0D (non-spatial) systems.

coupletype.description: Coupling type name. Informational label identifying this system's role in coupling.

parameters.description: Named parameters (rate constants, temperature, photolysis rates, etc.).

subsystems.description: Named child reaction systems (subsystems), keyed by unique identifier. Enables hierarchical system composition. Variables in subsystems are referenced via dot notation: "ParentSystem.ChildSystem.species". Each subsystem can be defined inline or included by reference via a local file path or URL.

tolerance.description: System-level default numerical tolerance for tests, used when a test or assertion does not provide its own.

tests.description: Inline validation tests that exercise this reaction system in isolation. Each test specifies initial conditions, parameter overrides, a time span, and scalar assertions at specific (species/variable, time) points.

examples.description: Inline illustrative examples of how to run this reaction system. Each example specifies initial state, parameters, a time span, an optional parameter sweep, and plot specifications.

Tolerance.description: Numerical comparison tolerance. Any of abs/rel may be specified. If both are given, an assertion passes when either bound is satisfied: |actual - expected| <= abs  OR  |actual - expected| / max(|expected|, epsilon) <= rel.

rel.description: Relative tolerance: |actual - expected| / max(|expected|, epsilon) <= rel.

Assertion.description: A single scalar check at a (variable, time) point.

variable.description: Name of the variable or species to check. Use the local name (e.g., "O3") or a scoped reference relative to this component (e.g., "subsystem.X").

time.description: Simulation time at which to evaluate the assertion. Must lie within [time_span.start, time_span.end].

expected.description: Expected scalar value of the variable at the given time.

tolerance.description: Per-assertion tolerance override. If present, this takes precedence over the test-level and model-level defaults.

Test.description: An inline validation test for the enclosing model or reaction system. Defines the run configuration (initial conditions, parameter overrides, time span) and the scalar assertions that must hold.

id.description: Identifier unique within this component's tests array.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this test verifies.

initial_conditions.description: Initial-value overrides for state variables, keyed by variable name (local to this component). Values not listed fall back to the variable's declared default.

parameter_overrides.description: Parameter overrides, keyed by parameter name (local to this component). Values not listed fall back to the parameter's declared default.

tolerance.description: Test-level default tolerance applied to all assertions in this test that do not override it.

assertions.description: Scalar (variable, time) checks that define the pass/fail criterion of the test.

TimeSpan.description: Simulation time interval expressed in the component's time units.

scale.description: Spacing: linear = evenly spaced between start and stop; log = logarithmically spaced (start and stop must be strictly positive).

SweepDimension.description: One axis of a parameter sweep: exactly one of values or range must be given.

parameter.description: Name of the parameter to vary (local to this component).

values.description: Enumerated values to use for this axis.

range.description: Generated range; mutually exclusive with values.

ParameterSweep.description: A parameter sweep specification. Currently only Cartesian product sweeps are supported — the total run count is the product of each dimension's length.

type.description: Sweep combination strategy. Currently only cartesian (full Cartesian product) is supported.

PlotAxis.description: Axis specification: any state variable, observed variable, parameter name, or swept parameter may be used.

variable.description: Variable or parameter name (local to this component or scoped within a subsystem).

label.description: Human-readable axis label. Viewers should fall back to the variable name if omitted.

PlotValue.description: A scalar value derived from a trajectory: used for heatmap z / color channels. Exactly one of at_time or reduce should be specified; if both are given, at_time takes precedence.

variable.description: Variable whose trajectory is reduced to a scalar per run.

at_time.description: Specific simulation time at which to sample the variable.

reduce.description: Time-reduction applied to the trajectory: max/min/mean over the run, time integral, or the final value at time_span.end.

PlotSeries.description: A single named series for multi-series line or scatter plots.

Plot.description: A plot specification associated with an example. Only structural information is recorded — axes, series selection, and value reductions. Styling (colors, fonts, legends, themes) is the viewer's concern.

id.description: Identifier unique within this example's plots array.

value.description: Required for heatmap; defines the color channel. Ignored for line/scatter.

series.description: Multiple named series for line or scatter plots. Ignored for heatmap.

Example.description: An inline illustrative example of how to run the enclosing component. Defines the run configuration and one or more plots derived from the result.

id.description: Identifier unique within this component's examples array.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this example illustrates.

initial_state.description: Initial conditions for state variables. Reuses the top-level InitialConditions $def (constant / per_variable / from_file).

parameters.description: Parameter overrides, keyed by parameter name (local to this component).

parameter_sweep.description: Optional parameter sweep. When present, the example represents a family of runs (one per Cartesian combination) rather than a single trajectory.

plots.description: Plot specifications derived from this example's run(s).

DataLoaderSource.description: File discovery configuration. Describes how to locate data files at runtime via URL templates with date/variable substitutions.

url_template.description: Jinja-style URL template with substitutions. Supported: {date:<strftime>} (e.g. {date:%Y%m%d}), {var}, {sector}, {species}. Custom substitutions are allowed and the runtime must accept and pass them through.

mirrors.description: Ordered fallback URL templates. Runtime tries each in order, first is primary. Follows the same substitution grammar as url_template.

DataLoaderTemporal.description: Temporal coverage and record layout for a data source.

start.description: ISO 8601 datetime — first timestamp available from this source.

end.description: ISO 8601 datetime — last timestamp available from this source.

file_period.description: ISO 8601 duration describing how much time one file covers (e.g., "P1D", "P1M", "PT3H").

frequency.description: ISO 8601 duration describing spacing between samples within a file.

records_per_file.description: Number of time records per file. "auto" means read from file at runtime.

time_variable.description: Name of the time coordinate variable in the file. Used when records_per_file is absent or "auto". If both static declarations (records_per_file + frequency) and time_variable are present, the static declaration wins and time_variable is a fallback.

DataLoaderSpatial.description: Spatial grid description for a data source.

crs.description: Coordinate reference system as a PROJ string or EPSG code.

grid_type.description: Structural grid family. Use "unstructured" for mesh/point datasets.

resolution.description: Per-dimension resolution in native CRS units. Optional; some datasets only know this at runtime.

extent.description: Per-dimension [min, max] extent in native CRS units. Optional; runtime can infer from files.

DataLoaderVariable.description: A variable exposed by a data loader, mapped from a source-file variable.

file_variable.description: Name of the variable inside the source file. May differ from the schema-level variable name.

units.description: Units of the variable as exposed to the schema.

unit_conversion.description: Optional multiplicative factor or Expression AST applied to convert source-file values to the declared units.

DataLoaderRegridding.description: Structural regridding configuration. Algorithm-specific tuning parameters are runtime-side and not in the schema.

fill_value.description: Value to assign to cells with no source data.

extrapolation.description: Behavior when regridding targets fall outside the source extent. Defaults to "clamp".

DataLoader.description: A generic, runtime-agnostic description of an external data source. Carries enough structural information to locate files, map timestamps to files, describe spatial/variable semantics, and regrid — rather than pointing at a runtime handler. Authentication and algorithm-specific tuning are runtime-only and not part of the schema.

kind.description: Structural kind of the dataset. Scientific role (emissions, meteorology, elevation, ...) is not schema-validated and belongs in metadata.tags.

variables.description: Variables exposed by this loader, keyed by schema-level variable name.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata about the data source. The "tags" field (array of strings) is conventional for expressing scientific role (e.g. "emissions", "reanalysis") and is not schema-validated.

Operator.description: A registered runtime operator (e.g., dry deposition, wet scavenging).

operator_id.description: Registered identifier the runtime uses to find the implementation.

TranslateTarget.description: Translation target: a simple variable reference string or an object with var and factor.

ConnectorEquation.description: A single equation in a ConnectorSystem linking two coupled systems.

transform.description: How the expression modifies the target.

CouplingEntry.description: A single coupling rule connecting models, reaction systems, data loaders, or operators.

CouplingOperatorCompose.description: Match LHS time derivatives and add RHS terms together.

translate.description: Variable mappings when LHS variables don't have matching names.

interface.description: Name of an interface from the interfaces section for cross-domain coupling.

lifting.description: Strategy for mapping between 0D and spatial systems.

CouplingCouple.description: Bi-directional coupling via explicit ConnectorSystem equations.

interface.description: Name of an interface from the interfaces section for cross-domain coupling.

lifting.description: Strategy for mapping between 0D and spatial systems.

CouplingVariableMap.description: Replace a parameter in one system with a variable from another.

interface.description: Name of an interface from the interfaces section for cross-domain coupling.

lifting.description: Strategy for mapping between 0D and spatial systems.

CouplingOperatorApply.description: Register an Operator to run during simulation.

operator.description: Name of the operator (key in the operators section).

CouplingCallback.description: Register a callback for simulation events.

CouplingEvent.description: Cross-system event involving variables from multiple coupled systems.

event_type.description: Whether this is a continuous or discrete event.

conditions.description: Condition expressions (zero-crossing for continuous, boolean for discrete).

affects.description: Affect equations. Required unless functional_affect is used.

functional_affect.description: Registered functional affect handler (alternative to symbolic affects).

SpatialDimension.description: Specification of a single spatial dimension.

BoundaryCondition.description: Boundary condition for one or more dimensions.

type.description: constant/dirichlet = fixed value; zero_gradient/neumann = ∂u/∂n = 0; periodic = wrap-around; robin = αu + β∂u/∂n = γ.

robin_alpha.description: Robin BC coefficient α for u term in αu + β∂u/∂n = γ.

robin_beta.description: Robin BC coefficient β for ∂u/∂n term in αu + β∂u/∂n = γ.

robin_gamma.description: Robin BC RHS value γ in αu + β∂u/∂n = γ.

spatial.description: Spatial dimensions, keyed by name (e.g., lon, lat, lev).

InterfaceConstraint.description: Constraint on a non-shared dimension at the interface.

value.description: Where the dimension is constrained: 'min', 'max', 'boundary', or a numeric coordinate.

Interface.description: Geometric connection between two domains of potentially different dimensionality.

domains.description: The two domains connected by this interface.

shared.description: Mapping of corresponding dimensions across domains, keyed by 'domain.dimension'.

constraints.description: Non-shared dimensions fixed at the interface, keyed by 'domain.dimension'.

regridding.description: Regridding strategy when shared dimensions differ in resolution.
